title: Transformers: BotBots
synopsis: When a bolt of Energon-infused lightning strikes a shopping mall, ordinary everyday objects spring to life as tiny transforming robots called BotBots. Organized into squads based on their store of origin, these miniature bots live a secret existence hidden from humans. A group of outcasts called the Lost Bots accidentally reveal themselves to Dave, the mall's night security guard, breaking the BotBots' most sacred rule. Now these lovable misfits must work together to hide their existence while trying to find acceptance among the mall's many factions.

episodes.title: Mall Than Meets the Eye / (Never) Be Yourself
episodes.synopsis: When lightning strikes the Bargain Box Mall, everyday objects transform into BotBots. The Lost Bots accidentally reveal themselves to security guard Dave, breaking the BotBots' sacred rule of secrecy. / The Lost Bots try to fit in with the mall's established squads but learn that pretending to be something you're not never works.

episodes.title: Escape from Snackatraz / I, Cheeseburger
episodes.synopsis: The Lost Bots attempt a daring escape from the food court's Hunger Hubs territory. / Burgertron has an existential crisis about his identity as a cheeseburger bot.

episodes.title: Phoning It In / The Lost Bots and the Claw Crusade
episodes.synopsis: A phone-based BotBot causes communication chaos throughout the mall. / The Lost Bots embark on a quest involving the mall's claw machine, facing rival squads along the way.

episodes.title: Live and Let LARP / Lone Bot and Carb
episodes.synopsis: The BotBots get involved in a live-action role-playing game that takes on a life of its own. / Burgertron goes solo but discovers that lone wolves have it tough in the mall.

episodes.title: Dimlit in Love / On the Bot Prom Dance Floor
episodes.synopsis: Dimlit develops a crush that complicates the Lost Bots' plans. / The BotBots throw a prom night dance that turns into a chaotic celebration.

episodes.title: The Ruckus Rally / Crime and Bun-ishment
episodes.synopsis: The Lost Bots enter a high-speed rally race through the mall's corridors. / A crime spree in the bakery section leads to a detective-style investigation.

episodes.title: Spirit of Halloween / Rage Against the Karaoke Machine
episodes.synopsis: A Halloween celebration at the mall brings spooky hijinks as BotBots compete for the best costume. / A karaoke machine BotBot goes haywire, turning the mall into a musical battleground.

episodes.title: Scanned Out / The Science Alliance
episodes.synopsis: A scanning device threatens to expose all BotBots to the outside world. / The Science Alliance squad conducts experiments that spiral out of control, endangering the whole mall.

episodes.title: Agent Smartlit / Shopping Brawl
episodes.synopsis: Dimlit goes undercover as a spy, adopting the persona of 'Agent Smartlit' to infiltrate a rival squad. / A disagreement between squads escalates into an all-out shopping brawl.

episodes.title: The Goldrush Games - Part the First / The Goldrush Games - Part the Second
episodes.synopsis: In the two-part season finale, all BotBot squads compete in the legendary Goldrush Games tournament. The Lost Bots must prove their worth against every squad in the mall, ultimately earning their place as a recognized faction through teamwork and heart.

facts: BotBots was the first Transformers cartoon to be a straight-up comedy with no action-adventure elements, focusing entirely on humor.
The series features a unique mixed-media visual style combining 2D puppet-rig animation for characters with semi-photorealistic cutout backgrounds.
The Canadian-based (Toronto) voice cast marked the first Transformers cartoon with a Canadian voice cast since 2005's Cybertron.
The series received three Industry Excellence Award nominations from the Manchester Animation Festival in November 2022.
'Crime and Bun-ishment' won both the Scriptwriting and Storyboarding awards at the Manchester Animation Festival.
Only one season of 10 episodes (20 eleven-minute segments) was produced, making it one of the shortest-running Transformers animated series.
The showrunners Kevin Burke and Chris Wyatt intentionally kept the series independent from the broader Transformers multiverse, positioning it as a standalone jumping-on point for new fans.
Character designs feature rounded, human-proportioned robots rather than the traditional angular Transformer aesthetic.
